title: Sort by Frequency and Value
description:
Given an array of integers, sort it in increasing order based on the frequency of each distinct value (how many times it appears in the array). If two values have the same frequency, sort them in decreasing order (larger value first).

Return the sorted array.

Constraints:
- 1 ≤ nums.length ≤ 10^5
- -10^4 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10^4

testCases.description: Mixed frequencies with tie-breaking by value descending

hints: Count the frequency of each element using a hash map first.
Use a custom comparator that sorts by frequency (ascending), then by value (descending).
In Python, use a tuple key like (freq[x], -x) to sort by frequency asc then value desc.

complexity.explanation: Building the frequency map takes O(n). Sorting with a custom comparator takes O(n log n).
